title: US official says shipments of Nvidia H200 chips to China have begun
gist: A senior US Commerce Department official confirmed that shipments of Nvidia's H200 AI chips to China and Hong Kong have begun under licenses granted to roughly 10 Chinese companies, though volumes to date are described as 'trivial,' drawing criticism from lawmakers concerned the Trump administration is loosening export controls.

themes.theme: H200 export licensing to China begins

insights.claim: Commerce Department Undersecretary Jeffrey Kessler told Congress that shipments of Nvidia H200 AI chips to China and Hong Kong have started, with the government having approved licenses for roughly 10 Chinese companies to purchase the processors, though the volume shipped to date is 'trivial.'
insights.speaker: B
insights.so_what: Confirms actual (not just approved) H200 shipments to China have begun, a concrete data point for tracking Nvidia's China revenue recovery and the pace of export-control loosening under the current administration.
insights.quote: He described the volume shipped to date as "trivial," underscoring that the transactions remain limited and closely scrutinized.

insights.claim: The limited H200 shipments mark a notable change from earlier in 2026, when U.S. officials said no H200 chips had yet reached Chinese customers, indicating export policy has shifted toward permitting some high-end AI chip sales to China.
insights.speaker: B
insights.so_what: A shift from zero to nonzero (even if small) H200 shipments is an early signal of policy loosening that could presage larger volume approvals, relevant to Nvidia's China-market revenue trajectory.
insights.quote: The limited shipments mark a notable change from earlier in 2026, when U.S. officials said no H200 chips had yet reached Chinese customers.

insights.claim: Representative Gregory Meeks, top Democrat on the House Foreign Affairs Committee, criticized the Commerce Department for not recently adding more Chinese companies to its Entity List, questioning whether the Trump administration is weakening export-control safeguards by permitting H200 exports.
insights.speaker: B
insights.so_what: Congressional pushback on export-control enforcement signals continued political risk/volatility around US-China AI chip policy that could affect Nvidia's ability to sustain or expand China shipments.
